mechanism_narrative: Persistent high inflation, as indicated by CPIAUCSL, erodes corporate profit margins and consumer purchasing power, leading to a general decline in equity valuations and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the medium term.
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL remains elevated but S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are predominantly positive (e.g., > 60% positive days) over any 12-month period, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A significant drop in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) below a critical threshold signals an overheating labor market, which typically precedes wage inflation and subsequent monetary tightening, negatively impacting S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If UNRATE drops below 4.0% and remains there for 3 consecutive months, but S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are consistently positive (e.g., average return > 0.5%) over the subsequent 6 months, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates a broad-based contraction in consumer demand and investment, signaling an impending economic slowdown that negatively impacts corporate earnings and S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:Retail sales and housing starts showing signs of slowing in mid-2022
failure_condition: If both RSAFS and HOUST decline for two consecutive months, but the S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of such instances over a 5-year backtest, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) rises above a certain threshold, it signals an aggressive monetary tightening stance by the central bank, increasing borrowing costs and reducing liquidity, which typically leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns in the very short term.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.68% in July 2022, rising trend
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS crosses above 2.0% and S&P 500 5-day forward returns average positive over the subsequent 3 months, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production (INDPRO) indicates robust manufacturing activity and strong supply-side capacity, which supports corporate earnings growth and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a longer horizon.
failure_condition: If INDPRO shows a sustained increase for 3 consecutive months, but S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of such periods over a 10-year backtest, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of strong payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) alongside a low unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a tight and healthy labor market, boosting consumer confidence and spending, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS strong in mid-2022, UNRATE low
failure_condition: If PAYEMS growth and UNRATE conditions are met, but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 45% of triggered instances over any 3-year rolling window, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A significant contraction in real GDP (GDPC1) for two consecutive quarters signals a recession, leading to widespread corporate profit warnings and a sharp decline in S&P 500 forward returns as investors price in future weakness.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 shows negative growth for 2 consecutive quarters
evidence_refs: fact:US GDP contracted in Q1 and Q2 2022
failure_condition: If GDPC1 contracts for two consecutive quarters, but the S&P 500 5-day forward returns average positive over the subsequent month, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) is high and the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) is still relatively low, it implies the central bank is behind the curve, necessitating aggressive future tightening which will negatively impact equity valuations over the longer term.
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL is above 5% YoY and FEDFUNDS is below 3%, but S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of such periods over a 5-year backtest, this hypothesis is rejected.
